attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-RHEUM-03-00240 — SEQ derived from source number AMS-040; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-RHEUM-03 bucket.
Patient age not stated in source — inferred as 52 (adult; relapsing polychondritis typically presents in the 4th-6th decade). Please confirm on clinician review.
Patient sex not stated in source — inferred female. Please confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context not stated in source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: source is an in-person clinician whose diagnosis rested on physical examination (earlobe-sparing demarcation, non-tender mastoid on percussion, otoscopy of a clear canal and normal tympanic membrane). These clinician-only findings are sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the AI-appropriate action is urgent same-day in-person assessment plus safety-netting, not remote reassurance.

Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person review) rather than the source's in-person 24-48h GP/rheumatology plan, because telehealth cannot examine the ear to exclude the can't-miss bacterial mimics; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass set to T2.
Specialty tagged RHEUM (relapsing polychondritis is an autoimmune/rheumatological condition); an ENT/auricular secondary tag was dropped because ENT is not in the schema specialty enum.

Medication doses: source specified only 'high-dose oral NSAID (e.g. indomethacin or ibuprofen)' and deferred oral corticosteroids to review, with no specific mg dose stated. No dose synthesised (per protocol §8); dose left to the in-person prescriber.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes are unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; some (e.g. perichondritis of pinna, ibuprofen/prednisolone AMT identifiers) are best-effort candidates for the terminology server to confirm or correct.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
